name: figjam-plugin
description: FigJam plugin development workflow. Use when working on plugin code (code.ts, ui.ts), debugging WebSocket communication, or building the plugin.
capabilities: Guide two-thread plugin development (main thread and UI thread)
Explain Figma API constraints for main thread code
Debug WebSocket communication between CLI and plugin
Build plugin with bun and import to Figma Desktop
Handle JSON import validation and normalization
Debug plugin using browser DevTools and Figma console
author: 7nohe
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing only markdown prompts and JSON metadata. No executable code, scripts, network calls, file system access, or external command execution. All 32 static findings are false positives triggered by markdown formatting (backticks in code blocks) and JSON hash strings. This is a pure prompt-based skill providing development guidance.
valueStatement: FigJam plugin development requires understanding Figma API constraints and WebSocket communication patterns. This skill provides guidance on building, debugging, and deploying plugins that render AWS architecture diagrams in FigJam canvases.
userTitle: Develop FigJam plugins with two-thread architecture
limitations: Requires Figma Desktop app, not available in browser
Main thread cannot use browser APIs (window, document, fetch)
WebSocket server must be running separately via CLI
Limited to Figma plugin API capabilities

useCases.title: Create AWS architecture diagrams
useCases.description: Build YAML-driven AWS diagrams that sync live to FigJam for team collaboration.

useCases.title: Set up FigJam plugin projects
useCases.description: Initialize and configure two-thread FigJam plugin with proper architecture.

useCases.title: Debug plugin communication
useCases.description: Diagnose WebSocket issues and connection problems between CLI and plugin UI.

promptTemplates.title: Initialize plugin project
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I set up a FigJam plugin with the two-thread architecture? Explain code.ts and ui.ts responsibilities.

promptTemplates.title: Debug WebSocket connection
promptTemplates.prompt: My FigJam plugin is not connecting to the WebSocket server. What steps do I take to debug the connection?

promptTemplates.title: Build and import plugin
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I build the FigJam plugin and import it into Figma Desktop? What is the manifest.json configuration?

promptTemplates.title: Handle main thread errors
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I debug errors in code.ts (main thread) that affect canvas rendering? What tools do I use?

bestPractices: Keep main thread code minimal and focused on canvas rendering
Validate and normalize data in UI thread before sending to main thread
Use browser DevTools for UI errors and Figma console for main thread errors
antiPatterns: Using browser APIs in code.ts (window, document, fetch) - will fail
Skipping validation of JSON input before processing
Building without testing in Figma Desktop environment

faq.question: What is the difference between code.ts and ui.ts in FigJam plugins?
faq.answer: code.ts runs on main thread with Figma API access only. ui.ts runs in iframe with browser APIs and WebSocket support.

faq.question: Can I use this plugin in Figma browser editor?
faq.answer: No. FigJam plugins require Figma Desktop app. Browser editor does not support custom plugin development.

faq.question: How do I debug WebSocket connection issues?
faq.answer: Open browser DevTools on plugin UI (right-click → Inspect) to see connection status and error messages in console.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this plugin?
faq.answer: Yes. Plugin runs locally with WebSocket server on localhost. No data is sent to external servers.

faq.question: Why is my code.ts failing with undefined errors?
faq.answer: Main thread cannot use browser APIs. Ensure no window, document, or fetch calls in code.ts. Use figma.* API only.

faq.question: How does this compare to other diagram tools?
faq.answer: This tool uses YAML for architecture definitions and syncs live to FigJam. Unlike draw.io, it offers version-controlled diagrams.
